bead.title: ci: add autonomous coverage, simulation, and benchmark ratchets
bead.description:
Context: TP-001 defines ratchets for line coverage, correctness seeds, invariant count, simulation hours, and performance p99. Current CI does not enforce coverage thresholds or benchmark ratchets, and long-running proof is not separated from per-commit proof.

In-scope files: CI workflow, existing test/benchmark scripts, and lightweight ratchet metadata files if the repo already has a pattern for them.
Out-of-scope: writing all missing product tests or inventing a large new benchmarking framework.
Implementation hint: add the smallest maintainable gate: per-commit scanner/coverage smoke where feasible, plus a documented nightly/manual command for criterion and extended `axon-sim` seeds if full enforcement would be too costly for PR CI.
bead.acceptance:
1. CI or a documented workflow runs the executable @covers scanner.
2. A reproducible command exists for bounded `axon-sim` seed execution and passes locally.
3. A reproducible command exists for benchmark/criterion execution or a precise blocker is recorded.
4. `cargo test -p axon-sim` passes.
5. The change documents which ratchets are enforced per commit versus nightly/manual.
